verses.text: In the morning they bind Jesus and deliver Him to Pilate. Handed over. The council passes Him to Rome for execution.
In the morning they bind Jesus and deliver Him to Pilate. Handed over. The council passes Him to Rome for execution.
Judas, repenting, returns the silver, 'I have betrayed... innocent blood,' then hangs himself. Remorse without hope. He owns his sin but flees to despair, not mercy.
Judas, repenting, returns the silver, 'I have betrayed... innocent blood,' then hangs himself. Remorse without hope. He owns his sin but flees to despair, not mercy.
Judas, repenting, returns the silver, 'I have betrayed... innocent blood,' then hangs himself. Remorse without hope. He owns his sin but flees to despair, not mercy.
The priests buy the potter's field with the blood-money, fulfilling prophecy. Scrupulous hypocrites. They strain over temple law while plotting murder.
The priests buy the potter's field with the blood-money, fulfilling prophecy. Scrupulous hypocrites. They strain over temple law while plotting murder.
The priests buy the potter's field with the blood-money, fulfilling prophecy. Scrupulous hypocrites. They strain over temple law while plotting murder.
The priests buy the potter's field with the blood-money, fulfilling prophecy. Scrupulous hypocrites. They strain over temple law while plotting murder.
The priests buy the potter's field with the blood-money, fulfilling prophecy. Scrupulous hypocrites. They strain over temple law while plotting murder.
Pilate asks, Art thou the King of the Jews? Jesus answers nothing to the charges, that the governor marvels. Silent dignity. He makes no defence against false accusation.
Pilate asks, Art thou the King of the Jews? Jesus answers nothing to the charges, that the governor marvels. Silent dignity. He makes no defence against false accusation.
Pilate asks, Art thou the King of the Jews? Jesus answers nothing to the charges, that the governor marvels. Silent dignity. He makes no defence against false accusation.
Pilate asks, Art thou the King of the Jews? Jesus answers nothing to the charges, that the governor marvels. Silent dignity. He makes no defence against false accusation.
Pilate offers to free Barabbas or Jesus, knowing they delivered Him 'for envy.' A telling choice. The crowd must choose between a murderer and the Saviour.
Pilate offers to free Barabbas or Jesus, knowing they delivered Him 'for envy.' A telling choice. The crowd must choose between a murderer and the Saviour.
Pilate offers to free Barabbas or Jesus, knowing they delivered Him 'for envy.' A telling choice. The crowd must choose between a murderer and the Saviour.
Pilate offers to free Barabbas or Jesus, knowing they delivered Him 'for envy.' A telling choice. The crowd must choose between a murderer and the Saviour.
His wife warns of her dream; but the priests sway the crowd to ask Barabbas. Conscience overruled. Pressure and warning alike are brushed aside.
His wife warns of her dream; but the priests sway the crowd to ask Barabbas. Conscience overruled. Pressure and warning alike are brushed aside.
His wife warns of her dream; but the priests sway the crowd to ask Barabbas. Conscience overruled. Pressure and warning alike are brushed aside.
What shall I do with Jesus? 'Let him be crucified.' What evil hath he done? They cry the more. Mob fury. They demand His death without a charge.
What shall I do with Jesus? 'Let him be crucified.' What evil hath he done? They cry the more. Mob fury. They demand His death without a charge.
Pilate washes his hands; they cry, 'His blood be on us'; he frees Barabbas and gives up Jesus. Vain washing. No water can cleanse the hands that condemn the innocent.
Pilate washes his hands; they cry, 'His blood be on us'; he frees Barabbas and gives up Jesus. Vain washing. No water can cleanse the hands that condemn the innocent.
Pilate washes his hands; they cry, 'His blood be on us'; he frees Barabbas and gives up Jesus. Vain washing. No water can cleanse the hands that condemn the innocent.
The soldiers mock Him with robe, thorns, and reed, 'Hail, King of the Jews,' then lead Him out. Cruel scorn. They jeer at the very kingship that is truly His.
The soldiers mock Him with robe, thorns, and reed, 'Hail, King of the Jews,' then lead Him out. Cruel scorn. They jeer at the very kingship that is truly His.
The soldiers mock Him with robe, thorns, and reed, 'Hail, King of the Jews,' then lead Him out. Cruel scorn. They jeer at the very kingship that is truly His.

The soldiers mock Him with robe, thorns, and reed, 'Hail, King of the Jews,' then lead Him out. Cruel scorn. They jeer at the very kingship that is truly His.
Simon is made to bear His cross; at Golgotha they offer vinegar mixed with gall. The bitter way. He treads the road of sorrow to the place of death.
Simon is made to bear His cross; at Golgotha they offer vinegar mixed with gall. The bitter way. He treads the road of sorrow to the place of death.
Simon is made to bear His cross; at Golgotha they offer vinegar mixed with gall. The bitter way. He treads the road of sorrow to the place of death.
They crucify Him, part His garments, and set over Him, 'THIS IS JESUS THE KING OF THE JEWS,' between two thieves. The cross. The King reigns from a tree, numbered with transgressors.
They crucify Him, part His garments, and set over Him, 'THIS IS JESUS THE KING OF THE JEWS,' between two thieves. The cross. The King reigns from a tree, numbered with transgressors.
They crucify Him, part His garments, and set over Him, 'THIS IS JESUS THE KING OF THE JEWS,' between two thieves. The cross. The King reigns from a tree, numbered with transgressors.
They crucify Him, part His garments, and set over Him, 'THIS IS JESUS THE KING OF THE JEWS,' between two thieves. The cross. The King reigns from a tree, numbered with transgressors.
Passers-by revile Him, 'If thou be the Son of God, come down'; the priests mock His saving others. Taunting unbelief. They scorn the very love that keeps Him there.
Passers-by revile Him, 'If thou be the Son of God, come down'; the priests mock His saving others. Taunting unbelief. They scorn the very love that keeps Him there.
Passers-by revile Him, 'If thou be the Son of God, come down'; the priests mock His saving others. Taunting unbelief. They scorn the very love that keeps Him there.
'He saved others; himself he cannot save'; the thieves cast it in His teeth. Misread weakness. His refusal to come down was His power to save.
'He saved others; himself he cannot save'; the thieves cast it in His teeth. Misread weakness. His refusal to come down was His power to save.
'He saved others; himself he cannot save'; the thieves cast it in His teeth. Misread weakness. His refusal to come down was His power to save.
Darkness covers the land; Jesus cries, 'My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?' The dread cry. He bears the forsaking our sin deserved.
Darkness covers the land; Jesus cries, 'My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?' The dread cry. He bears the forsaking our sin deserved.
Darkness covers the land; Jesus cries, 'My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?' The dread cry. He bears the forsaking our sin deserved.
One offers vinegar; Jesus cries again with a loud voice and 'yielded up the ghost.' Willing death. He lays down His life of His own accord.
One offers vinegar; Jesus cries again with a loud voice and 'yielded up the ghost.' Willing death. He lays down His life of His own accord.
One offers vinegar; Jesus cries again with a loud voice and 'yielded up the ghost.' Willing death. He lays down His life of His own accord.
The temple veil is rent, the earth quakes, and graves open as saints arise. Opened way. His death tears the veil and breaks the power of the grave.
The temple veil is rent, the earth quakes, and graves open as saints arise. Opened way. His death tears the veil and breaks the power of the grave.
The temple veil is rent, the earth quakes, and graves open as saints arise. Opened way. His death tears the veil and breaks the power of the grave.
The centurion confesses, 'Truly this was the Son of God'; women watch from afar. Gentile witness. Even a pagan soldier owns Him at the cross.
The centurion confesses, 'Truly this was the Son of God'; women watch from afar. Gentile witness. Even a pagan soldier owns Him at the cross.
The centurion confesses, 'Truly this was the Son of God'; women watch from afar. Gentile witness. Even a pagan soldier owns Him at the cross.
Joseph of Arimathaea lays the body in his new tomb and rolls a stone; the women watch. Honoured burial. A secret disciple gives Him an honourable grave.
Joseph of Arimathaea lays the body in his new tomb and rolls a stone; the women watch. Honoured burial. A secret disciple gives Him an honourable grave.

Joseph of Arimathaea lays the body in his new tomb and rolls a stone; the women watch. Honoured burial. A secret disciple gives Him an honourable grave.
The priests seal the tomb and set a watch, lest the disciples steal Him. Guarded grave. Their own precaution would soon prove the resurrection real.
